name: Catalog and Publishing
description: M4 plumbing: lint a Flow Package against §7, publish it to npm, generate and host the catalog registry.json, and stand up the static catalog site at flows.relay.dev. Also include the telemetry opt-in plumbing the spec promises in §8.4.

waves.name: Flow package linter
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/lint.ts` exporting `lintFlowPackage(dir: string): Promise<LintReport>`. Checks per §7: (1) package.json exists with required fields (name, version semver, type:module, main, relay metadata block with displayName+tags+estimatedCostUsd+estimatedDurationMin+audience), (2) flow.ts OR dist/flow.js present, loadable, default-exports a Flow, (3) README.md contains the §7.4 ordered sections (grep for the heading strings) — missing 1–5 is an ERROR, missing 6–8 is a WARN, (4) prompts/ directory exists if any step.prompt references promptFile, (5) schemas/ files compile cleanly. Returns { errors, warnings } — used by publish and optionally by a GitHub Action in the catalog repo.

waves.name: registry.json generator
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/registry.ts` exporting `generateRegistryJson(packages: string[]): Promise<RegistryDoc>`. For each input (either local flow dir or npm package name), extract the `relay` metadata block + name + version + description + README excerpt and emit an entry conforming to a documented `Registry` type (version: 1, generatedAt, flows: [{ name, version, displayName, description, tags, audience, estimatedCostUsd, estimatedDurationMin, repoUrl?, npmPackage, readmeExcerpt }]). This is the file served at flows.relay.dev/registry.json — the static site (task_75) reads it; `relay search` (task_55) reads it. Include a CLI entry wrapper `bin/generate-registry.js` so the catalog repo's CI can invoke it.

waves.name: Telemetry client (opt-in)
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/telemetry.ts` per §8.4. Exports `maybeSendRunEvent(evt: RunEvent): Promise<void>` and `isEnabled()` reading `~/.relay/config.json` (`telemetry.enabled` boolean; default false). On enabled, POST the exact payload shape from §8.4 to `https://telemetry.relay.dev/runs` with a 2s timeout, swallow failures silently, no retries. Also create `packages/cli/src/commands/config.ts` with subcommands `config get <key>` and `config set <key> <value>` so the user can opt in. Wire `config` into the dispatcher. Call `maybeSendRunEvent` at the end of the `run` command (task_52) with the exact fields listed: flowName, flowVersion, status, durationMs, stepsCount, totalCostUsd, relayVersion, nodeVersion, platform. No input data, no prompts.

waves.name: relay publish
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/publish.ts` per §9.4. Accepts a flow package dir. Actions: (1) run `lintFlowPackage(dir)` (task_72) — fail on any ERROR, warn on WARN, (2) run `npm run build` inside the package if a build script exists, (3) run `npm publish --access public` in the dir, (4) on success, run `generateRegistryJson` against the updated set (local config points at the catalog repo's flow list) and print the registry diff. Support `--dry-run` that skips the actual publish but performs every other step. Print clear output with every command it runs so publication is auditable.

waves.name: Static catalog site
waves.description: Create a static site in `catalog/` (new top-level dir — update pnpm-workspace.yaml if needed). Use a minimal approach: plain HTML + a tiny JS file that fetches `/registry.json` and renders the flow list (name, displayName, version, tags, cost/duration, install command, link to README rendered from readmeExcerpt). No framework. Include Tailwind via CDN or inline CSS. Build script = 'echo static site; exit 0' (no build needed). Add a GitHub Actions workflow file `.github/workflows/catalog-deploy.yml` that runs `generate-registry.js` on push and deploys `catalog/` + the generated registry.json to GitHub Pages (or the Ganderbite-chosen host). Include placeholder env-var docs for the deploy target.
